prose: Fukui reactivity analysis of benzaldehyde was performed using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (tier 'standard') in the gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1, via PySCF (exact four-center integrals, no density fitting). Three single-point SCFs were run (neutral N, cation N−1 doublet, anion N+1 doublet) and Mulliken population analysis was used to compute per-atom f+, f−, f0, and dual descriptor. All SCFs converged; integrity.trustworthy = true. Fukui charge sums: sum(f+) = 1.0000, sum(f-) = 1.0000. No engine warnings reported. Headline result: most electrophilic atom is C3 with f+ = 0.39938967; most nucleophilic atom is O1 with f− = 0.23839906. Full per-atom table reported above. Plot at: /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/benzaldehyde/argo_claude-sonnet-4.6/20260706-173145_benzaldehyde_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01_plot.png
